evals.prompt: What cool new features has Google added to Skia that we're missing in SkiaSharp? We're on milestone 133.
evals.expected_output: Full scan report covering M79-M148. Should find SkMesh, gradient interpolation, SkImageFilters::RuntimeShader, HDR metadata, SkPath immutability, and other high-value gaps. Should produce JSON + HTML + markdown summary.
expectations: Produces a JSON file that passes validate-feature-scout.py (exit 0)
Produces an HTML viewer file
JSON meta.mode is 'full'
JSON meta.currentMilestone is 133
JSON items array contains at least 20 features
At least one item has name containing 'Mesh' or 'SkMesh' with bindingStatus 'missing' and priority 'high'
At least one item mentions gradient interpolation or color space interpolation with priority 'high'
At least one item mentions SkPath immutability or SkPathBuilder with priority 'critical' or 'high'
At least one item mentions RuntimeShader in image filters with bindingStatus 'missing'
Items with bindingStatus 'full' have cApiStatus 'present' and csharpStatus 'present'
JSON summary.totalItems equals the actual length of the items array
JSON nextSteps array is non-empty and first item has severity 'critical' or 'high'
nextSteps include at least one entry with skillToUse field
Markdown summary includes a 'Before vs After' or timeline section splitting features by milestone
Markdown summary includes a 'Quick Wins' section for partial-status items

evals.prompt: I'm about to bump Skia from m132 to m133. Can you scout the release notes and tell me what changed in this window?
evals.expected_output: Windowed scan for m132→m133. Should find SkColorSpace::MakeCICP as the key new feature (already bound). Should flag GPU timing and canvas save prealloc. Much smaller item count than full scan.
expectations: Produces a JSON file that passes validate-feature-scout.py (exit 0)
Produces an HTML viewer file
JSON meta.mode is 'windowed'
JSON meta.previousMilestone is 132
JSON meta.currentMilestone is 133
JSON items array has at least 3 items
At least one item mentions MakeCICP or CICP with bindingStatus 'full'
JSON summary counts are internally consistent (totalItems = sum of status counts)
Items are scoped to the m132-m133 window (milestoneIntroduced between 132 and 133 inclusive, or slightly wider for context)

evals.prompt: scout for new Skia features — especially anything related to HDR, color management, or image formats we should support
evals.expected_output: Full scan with emphasis on HDR/color/codec features. Should prominently feature skhdr::Metadata, skhdr::Agtm, CICP color spaces, AVIF, JPEG XL, new color types (R16), and gradient interpolation color spaces.
expectations: Produces a JSON file that passes validate-feature-scout.py (exit 0)
At least one item mentions HDR metadata or skhdr::Metadata
At least one item mentions AGTM or Adaptive Global Tone Mapping
At least one item mentions AVIF with category 'codec'
At least one item mentions CICP or MakeCICP
At least one item mentions R16 or kR16 color type
At least one item mentions gradient interpolation color spaces
Items related to HDR/color have priority 'high' or 'medium' (not 'low')
Markdown summary highlights HDR and color features prominently

evals.prompt: check what new Skia APIs we might be missing — also look at the C++ headers for SkImage and SkCanvas to see if there are hidden methods we haven't wrapped
evals.expected_output: Full scan plus C++ header discovery phase. Should find release-notes features AND discover hidden methods from upstream headers not in the C API. hiddenApis array should be non-empty.
expectations: Produces a JSON file that passes validate-feature-scout.py (exit 0)
JSON hiddenApis array has at least 1 entry
Hidden API entries have cppClass field mentioning 'SkImage' or 'SkCanvas'
Hidden API entries have cppHeader field pointing to an include/ path
JSON summary.hiddenApiCount matches the length of hiddenApis array
Regular items array also present with release-notes-based features
Markdown summary includes a 'Hidden APIs' section

evals.prompt: what performance improvements has Skia made recently that would benefit our users?
evals.expected_output: Should identify performance-related items from release notes. Should include Perlin noise raster improvement (M124), any codec decode optimizations, GPU backend performance changes. Performance array in JSON should be populated.
expectations: Produces a JSON file that passes validate-feature-scout.py (exit 0)
JSON performance array has at least 1 entry
At least one performance entry mentions Perlin noise or noise shader improvement
Performance entries have bindingActionNeeded boolean field
JSON summary.performanceCount matches the length of performance array
Markdown summary includes a performance or optimization section
